sentences.base_language: Minho had been looking forward to the weekend hike for months and woke up early Saturday morning feeling energised and excited.
sentences.target_language: 민호는 몇 달째 주말 등산을 손꼽아 기다려 왔고, 토요일 아침 일찍 일어났을 때 활기차고 설레는 기분이었다.
sentences.romanized: Minho-neun myeot daljjae jumal deungsan-eul sonkkoba gidaryeo watgo, toyoil achim illijjik ireonasseul ttae hwalgichago seolleneun gibun-ieotda.
literal: had been eagerly looking forward to-and,
was in a mood of being energised and excited.
sentences.grammarNote:
**-아/어 오다** continuing up to now: 기다려 **왔고** (had been looking forward to [and still is]). **-아/어 오다** marks an action or state that began in the past and has continued right up to the present: 살아 **왔다** (has been living), 노력해 **왔다** (has been making efforts). Combined with **몇 달째** (for several months now) it emphasises the sustained anticipation over a long period.
**몇 달째** duration with **-째**: **몇 달째** (for months now / for the nth month). The suffix **-째** attached to a counter expresses 'for [this many] units continuously without interruption': 사흘**째** (for three days running), 몇 주**째** (for weeks now). It carries the nuance that the situation has persisted without a break up to the present moment.

sentences.base_language: He packed his backpack carefully with water, kimbap, a rain jacket, and a small first-aid kit.
sentences.target_language: 그는 물, 김밥, 우비, 그리고 작은 구급 용품을 꼼꼼하게 배낭에 챙겨 넣었다.
sentences.romanized: Geu-neun mul, gimbap, ubi, geurigo jageun gugeup yongpum-eul kkomkkomhage baenang-e chaenggyeo neoheotda.
literal: 물, 김밥, 우비, 그리고 작은 구급 용품을
water, kimbap, a rain jacket, and a small first-aid kit-OBJ
sentences.grammarNote:
**챙기다** = to take care to bring, to pack: **챙겨 넣었다** (packed [it] in / carefully stowed). **챙기다** implies attentive, deliberate gathering of items — more intentional than simply 넣다 (to put in). As a compound **챙겨 넣다** it adds the nuance of placing each item thoughtfully: 도시락을 **챙겨** 가다 (take a packed lunch along).
**꼼꼼하게** adverb of manner: **꼼꼼하게** 챙겨 넣었다 (packed carefully / with great thoroughness). **꼼꼼하다** (to be meticulous, thorough) + **-게** (adverbialiser) → **꼼꼼하게** (meticulously). It is a mimetic-derived adjective describing careful, detail-oriented behaviour, and suits the deliberate preparation of a seasoned hiker: **꼼꼼하게** 확인하다 (check thoroughly).

sentences.base_language: The trailhead was about an hour's drive from Seoul and he arrived just as the sun was beginning to appear over the mountains.
sentences.target_language: 등산로 입구는 서울에서 차로 약 한 시간 거리였고, 해가 산 너머로 막 떠오르기 시작할 때쯤 도착했다.
sentences.romanized: Deungsanno ipgu-neun Seoul-eseo charo yak han sigan geori-yeotgo, hae-ga san neomeo-ro mak tteooreogi sijakhal ttaejjeum dochakaetda.
literal: 서울에서 차로 약 한 시간 거리였고,
was about an hour's drive from Seoul-and,
just around when [it] was beginning to rise
sentences.grammarNote:
**-기 시작하다** beginning of an action: 떠오르**기 시작할** 때쯤 (just around when [it] was beginning to rise). **-기 시작하다** marks the onset of an action: 비가 오**기 시작했다** (it began to rain), 이해하**기 시작했다** (began to understand). Here it is embedded in a future-form modifier **-ㄹ 때쯤** to express the approximate moment of onset from the narrator's past perspective.
**때쯤** approximate time: **-ㄹ 때쯤** (around the time when). **쯤** attached to a time expression softens it to an approximation: 세 시**쯤** (around three o'clock), 도착할 **때쯤** (around the time of arriving). It is a natural hedge that suits the imprecise, unhurried quality of an early morning arrival in the mountains.

sentences.base_language: The forest at the start of the trail was cool and peaceful, with birdsong filling the morning air.
sentences.target_language: 등산로 초입의 숲은 시원하고 고요했으며, 새소리가 아침 공기를 가득 채우고 있었다.
sentences.romanized: Deungsanno choib-eui sup-eun siwonhago goyohaetseumyeo, saesori-ga achim gonggi-reul gadeuk chaeuge isseotda.
literal: the forest at the start of the trail-TOPIC
sentences.grammarNote:
**-으며** formal written connector: 시원하고 고요했**으며** (was cool and peaceful-and). **-으며** is the literary/formal equivalent of **-고**, linking clauses or predicates in a list: 맑**으며** 깨끗하다 (is clear and clean). Its slightly elevated register sets a serene, descriptive tone fitting a nature scene, distinguishing written narrative prose from casual speech.
**가득 채우다** = to fill completely: 새소리가 공기를 **가득 채우고 있었다** (birdsong was filling the air completely). **가득** (full / to the brim) intensifies the verb **채우다** (to fill): 마음이 기쁨으로 **가득 찼다** (the heart was full of joy). The progressive **-고 있었다** sustains the image — birdsong was actively, continuously filling the morning air at that moment.

sentences.base_language: After about thirty minutes of walking, the path grew steeper and his breathing became noticeably heavier.
sentences.target_language: 약 삼십 분쯤 걷다 보니 길이 점점 가팔라지기 시작했고, 숨도 눈에 띄게 가빠지고 있었다.
sentences.romanized: Yak samsip bunjjeum geodda boni gil-i jeomjeom gapallajigi sijakhaetgo, sum-do nun-e ttwige gappajigo isseotda.
literal: as [he] kept walking for about thirty minutes (discovery in progress)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-다 보니** discovery through ongoing action: 걷**다 보니** 길이 가팔라졌다 (as [he] kept walking he found the path had grown steeper). **-다 보니** combines the mid-action **-다** with **보니** (having seen / having found) to describe a realisation that emerges naturally from continued activity: 하**다 보니** 재미있어졌다 (kept doing it and found it had become fun). It captures the gradual, incidental nature of the discovery.
**-아/어지다** change-of-state: 가팔라지**기 시작했고** (began to grow steeper) / 가빠지**고 있었다** (was becoming heavier). **-아/어지다** attached to an adjective stem creates a change-of-state verb: 어려워**지다** (become difficult), 따뜻해**지다** (become warm). Combined with **점점** (gradually) and progressive **-고 있었다** it renders the slow, incremental nature of the physical change vividly.

sentences.base_language: He stopped briefly to drink some water and take in the view of the valley spreading out below him.
sentences.target_language: 잠시 멈춰 서서 물을 마시고, 아래로 펼쳐지는 계곡의 풍경을 눈에 담았다.
sentences.romanized: Jamsi meomchwo seoseo mul-eul masigo, arae-ro pyeolchyeojineun gyegok-eui punggyeong-eul nun-e damatda.
literal: took in / held in the eyes.
sentences.grammarNote:
**눈에 담다** = to take in visually, to drink in with the eyes: **눈에 담았다** (held [the view] in his eyes / took it in). **담다** (to hold, to contain) + **눈에** (in the eyes): this idiomatic compound expresses the act of deliberately absorbing a sight — more immersive than simply 보다 (to look): 아름다운 경치를 눈에 **담다** (take in a beautiful scene). It conveys that he was consciously savouring the view.
**펼쳐지다** = to spread out, to unfurl: 아래로 **펼쳐지는** 계곡 (the valley spreading out below). **펼치다** (to spread/unfold) → passive **펼쳐지다** (to be spread out / to spread). As a present modifier **펼쳐지는** it paints the valley as a live, unfolding panorama: 눈앞에 **펼쳐지는** 풍경 (the scenery unfolding before one's eyes). The passive construction subtly removes human agency — the valley simply opens up.

sentences.base_language: A fellow hiker coming back down warned him there was a slippery section near the stream crossing ahead.
sentences.target_language: 내려오고 있던 등산객 한 명이 앞쪽 개울 건너는 곳 근처에 미끄러운 구간이 있다고 주의를 줬다.
sentences.romanized: Naeryeogo itdeon deungsangaek han myeong-i apjjok gaeul geonneoneun got geuncheo-e mikkeureoaneun gugan-i itdago juui-reul jwotda.
literal: who was coming back down (past progressive modifier)
that there was a slippery section (quoted content)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-고 있던** past progressive modifier: 내려**오고 있던** 등산객 (a fellow hiker who was coming down). **-고 있다** (progressive) + **-던** (retrospective modifier): **-고 있던** modifies a noun to describe someone who was in the middle of an action at a past reference point: 공부하**고 있던** 학생 (the student who was studying). It distinguishes an ongoing process at that moment from a completed action.
**주의를 주다** = to give a warning, to caution: **주의를 줬다** (gave a warning). **주의** (caution/attention) + **주다** (to give): this set phrase is the natural Korean expression for alerting someone to a hazard: **주의를 받다** (to receive a warning), **주의를 기울이다** (to pay attention). It is more specific than simply 말하다 (to say) and implies a helpful, safety-focused communication.

sentences.base_language: Minho thanked them and continued carefully, picking his way across the wet stones with his arms out for balance.
sentences.target_language: 민호는 고맙다고 인사를 하고 나서, 팔을 벌려 균형을 잡으면서 젖은 돌 위를 조심조심 건넜다.
sentences.romanized: Minho-neun gomapda-go insa-reul hago naseo, pal-eul beollyeo gyunhyeong-eul jabeuumyeonseo jeojeun dol wi-reul josimjosim geonneotda.
sentences.grammarNote:
**-면서** simultaneous manner: 균형을 잡으**면서** 건넜다 (crossed while keeping balance). **-면서** shows two concurrent actions by the same subject: 음악을 들으**면서** 걷다 (walk while listening to music). Here it links the physical act of balancing — arms out — with the act of crossing, painting a single composite image of careful, tentative progress across slippery stones.
**조심조심** reduplication as adverb of manner: **조심조심** 건넜다 (crossed very carefully / step by careful step). **조심** (caution) reduplicated → **조심조심** intensifies and makes the carefulness feel repeated and deliberate: **천천히** (slowly) vs **조심조심** (with each step taken cautiously). Korean reduplication of manner adverbs is a vivid stylistic device that captures a quality repeated at every moment of the action.

sentences.base_language: The middle section of the trail was the hardest — a long exposed ridge with a cold and relentless wind.
sentences.target_language: 등산로의 중간 구간이 가장 힘들었다. 차갑고 쉴 새 없이 불어오는 바람이 몰아치는 긴 능선이었다.
sentences.romanized: Deungsanno-eui junggan gugan-i gajang himdeureotda. Chagapgo swil sae eopsi bureooneun baram-i mollachineun gin neungseonigeotda.
literal: the middle section of the trail-SUBJECT
blowing relentlessly without a moment's rest (modifier)
sentences.grammarNote:
**쉴 새 없이** = without a moment's rest, relentlessly: **쉴 새 없이** 불어오는 바람 (wind that blows without a moment's pause). **쉬다** (to rest) + **-(으)ㄹ 새** (a moment/gap to do) + **없이** (without): literally 'without a gap to rest'. This idiomatic expression captures ceaseless, relentless activity: **쉴 새 없이** 말하다 (talk non-stop). It is far more vivid than simply 계속 (continuously).
**몰아치다** = to lash, to batter, to drive hard: 바람이 **몰아치는** 능선 (a ridge where the wind lashes). **몰다** (to drive/herd) + **치다** (to strike): a compound verb describing fierce, aggressive weather — wind, rain, or snow that beats and pushes relentlessly. As a present modifier **몰아치는** it sustains the sense of ongoing, unceasing assault on the exposed ridge.

sentences.base_language: By the time he reached the summit, his legs were burning and his clothes were soaked with sweat.
sentences.target_language: 정상에 도착했을 때쯤 다리는 불타는 듯했고, 옷은 땀으로 흠뻑 젖어 버렸다.
sentences.romanized: Jeongsang-e dochakaesseul ttaejjeum dari-neun bultaneun deuthaetgo, ot-eun ttam-euro heuMppyeok jeoeo beoryeotda.
literal: around the time [he] reached the summit
sentences.grammarNote:
**-는 듯하다** simile/conjecture: 불타**는 듯했다** (seemed as if [they] were burning). **-(으)ㄴ/는 듯하다** expresses a strong resemblance or inference: 아픈 **듯했다** (seemed to be in pain), 비가 올 **듯하다** (looks as though it will rain). Here it renders the burning sensation in the legs vividly without stating it as bare fact — a subjective, experiential description.
**-아/어 버리다** completion with finality: 젖어 **버렸다** (became soaked — completely and irreversibly). **-아/어 버리다** marks an action as fully completed, often with a sense of resignation or surprise at the result: 다 먹어 **버렸다** (ate it all up), 잊어 **버렸다** (forgot completely). Here it conveys that the saturation was total — nothing could be done about it — before he even reached the top.

sentences.base_language: But the view was worth every difficult step — a wide panorama of mountains and valleys stretching to the horizon.
sentences.target_language: 하지만 그 풍경은 힘들었던 모든 발걸음이 충분히 가치 있을 만했다. 수평선까지 뻗어 있는 산과 계곡의 넓은 파노라마였다.
sentences.romanized: Hajiman geu punggyeong-eun himdeureotdeon modeun balgeoreum-i chungbunhi gachi isseul manhaetda. Supyeongseeon-kkaji bbeodeo inneun san-gwa gyegok-eui neolbeun panorama-yeotda.
literal: stretching to the horizon (resultant state modifier)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-(으)ㄹ 만하다** worthwhile / worth doing: 가치 있**을 만했다** (was worth it / was worthy enough). **-(으)ㄹ 만하다** expresses that something is sufficiently worthwhile or deserving of the effort: 볼 **만하다** (worth seeing), 먹을 **만하다** (worth eating). Combined with **충분히** (sufficiently / well) it amplifies the judgement — every hard step was thoroughly justified by the view.
**뻗어 있다** resultant state of extension: **뻗어 있는** 파노라마 (a panorama stretching [and remaining stretched]). **뻗다** (to stretch/extend) + **-어 있다** (resultant state): the mountains and valleys had extended to the horizon and remained extended — a static, sweeping image. Compare: **뻗었다** (stretched — event), **뻗어 있다** (is stretched out — ongoing state). This distinction is essential for describing landscapes in Korean.

sentences.base_language: He sat on a flat rock, ate his kimbap, and let the silence and the scenery settle over him.
sentences.target_language: 그는 평평한 바위에 앉아 김밥을 먹으면서, 고요함과 풍경이 온몸에 스며드는 것을 느꼈다.
sentences.romanized: Geu-neun pyeongpyeonghan bawi-e anja gimbap-eul meogeumyeonseo, goyoham-gwa punggyeong-i onmom-e seumeodeuneun geot-eul neukkkeotda.
literal: the thing of seeping into his whole body (nominalised)
sentences.grammarNote:
**스며들다** = to seep into, to permeate: 온몸에 **스며드는** 것을 느꼈다 (felt the silence and scenery seeping into his whole body). **스미다** (to soak in) + **들다** (to enter): a compound verb describing gradual, deep permeation — of liquid, cold, or feeling: 추위가 **스며든다** (the cold seeps in), 감동이 가슴에 **스며들었다** (emotion permeated the heart). It renders the meditative absorption of the moment far more viscerally than simple 느끼다.
**-(으)ㄴ/는 것을 느끼다** = to feel that, to sense: **스며드는 것을 느꼈다** (felt [the act of] seeping in). **-(으)ㄴ/는 것** nominalises a verb clause — turning an action into a 'thing' that can be the object of 느끼다: 심장이 뛰**는 것을** 느꼈다 (felt the heart beating). The construction allows a physical or perceptual experience to be expressed with precise grammatical clarity.

sentences.base_language: Minho took dozens of photos, knowing the images would never fully capture what he was seeing.
sentences.target_language: 민호는 사진을 수십 장 찍으면서도, 그 이미지들이 눈앞의 광경을 절대로 온전히 담아낼 수 없을 것임을 알고 있었다.
sentences.romanized: Minho-neun sajin-eul susip jang jjigeumyeonseo-do, geu imijideul-i nun ap-eui gwanggyeong-eul jeoldaero onjeonhi damanael su eopseul geosiim-eul algo isseotda.
literal: even while taking dozens of photos-while,
that it would be impossible to fully capture (nominalised future)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-(으)ㄹ 수 없다** impossibility: **담아낼 수 없을 것임을** (that it would be impossible to fully capture). **-(으)ㄹ 수 없다** expresses inability or impossibility: 이해할 수 **없다** (cannot understand), 막을 수 **없다** (cannot stop). The future nominal form **-(으)ㄹ 것임** nominalises the prediction: '(the fact) that it would be impossible' — a grammatically dense construction typical of reflective, literate Korean prose.
**담아내다** = to capture, to contain and bring out: 광경을 **담아낼 수 없다** (cannot fully capture the scene). **담다** (to hold/contain) + **내다** (to bring out / to manage to): a compound verb implying the effort to hold something fully and faithfully: 감동을 글로 **담아내다** (capture emotion in writing). The **내다** component adds the nuance of successfully achieving a challenging task — here negated to show the camera's fundamental inadequacy.

sentences.base_language: A couple nearby told him there was a beautiful waterfall just twenty minutes off the main trail.
sentences.target_language: 근처에 있던 커플이, 주 등산로에서 불과 이십 분 거리에 아름다운 폭포가 있다고 알려 줬다.
sentences.romanized: Geuncheo-e itdeon keopeul-i, ju deungsanno-eseo bulgwa isip bun geori-e areumdaun pokpo-ga itdago allyeo jwotda.
literal: that there was a beautiful waterfall (quoted content)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-아/어 주다** benefactive: 알려 **줬다** (told [him] / let [him] know — as a favour). **알리다** (to inform) + **-아/어 주다** (do for someone's benefit): the couple shared information for Minho's benefit. **-아/어 주다** marks that the action is directed toward helping another: 가르쳐 **주다** (teach [for someone]), 열어 **주다** (open [for someone]). It adds warmth and social orientation to the act of sharing information.
**불과** = only, merely, just: **불과** 이십 분 거리 (just twenty minutes away). **불과** (不過) placed before a quantity emphasises how small or close something is — often with a slight sense of surprise: **불과** 사흘 만에 (in just three days), **불과** 몇 명 (merely a few people). Here it makes the detour sound temptingly easy — only twenty minutes off the path.

sentences.base_language: He decided it was well worth the extra effort and set off down the narrow side path.
sentences.target_language: 추가로 들이는 수고가 충분히 가치 있을 것이라고 생각해서, 좁은 옆 길을 따라 내려가기로 했다.
sentences.romanized: Chugaro deuriineun sugo-ga chungbunhi gachi isseul geosirago saenggakhaeseo, jobeun yeok gil-eul ttara naeryeogagi-ro haetda.
literal: that it would be well worth it (quoted future)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-기로 하다** decision: **내려가기로 했다** (decided to head down). **-기로 하다** is the standard construction for expressing a decision or resolution: 공부하**기로 했다** (decided to study), 일찍 오**기로 했다** (decided to come early). It differs from **-(으)려고 하다** (intention/plan) in that **-기로 하다** implies the decision has been made and committed to.
**-아/어서** cause leading to action: 생각**해서** 내려가기로 했다 (thought [it worthwhile] and so decided to go). **-아/어서** links a reason or prior action to its consequence: 피곤**해서** 쉬었다 (was tired so rested). Here it shows that the decision flowed directly from his assessment — the thinking caused the deciding. This causal chain is more immediate and personal than the formal **-(으)므로**.

sentences.base_language: The waterfall was spectacular — white water falling into a clear pool surrounded by green moss and ferns.
sentences.target_language: 폭포는 정말 장관이었다. 하얀 물줄기가 초록빛 이끼와 양치류로 둘러싸인 맑은 웅덩이로 떨어지고 있었다.
sentences.romanized: Pokpo-neun jeongmal jangwan-ieotda. Hayan muljulgi-ga chorokbit ikki-wa yangchiryu-ro dulleossain malgeun ungdeong-i-ro tteoreojigo isseotda.
sentences.grammarNote:
**둘러싸이다** = to be surrounded: 이끼와 양치류로 **둘러싸인** 웅덩이 (a pool surrounded by moss and ferns). **둘러싸다** (to surround) → passive **둘러싸이다** (to be surrounded). As a past modifier **둘러싸인** it describes the pool's permanent, encircling framing of vegetation. Korean passive constructions with **-이/히/리/기** are common for describing scenes where the noun is in a resultant state caused by external forces.
**물줄기** = a stream of water, a jet / flow of water: **하얀 물줄기** (a stream of white water). **물** (water) + **줄기** (stem / line / stream): a compound noun that evokes a distinct, flowing ribbon of water — more visual and dynamic than simply **물** (water). It is the natural Korean term for the column of a waterfall: 가는 **물줄기** (a thin stream of water), 힘찬 **물줄기** (a powerful torrent).

sentences.base_language: He stood there for a long time, feeling completely still and at peace.
sentences.target_language: 그는 오랫동안 그 자리에 서서, 완전히 고요하고 평온한 기분을 느끼고 있었다.
sentences.romanized: Geu-neun oraetdongan geu jari-e seoseo, wanjeonhi goyohago pyeongonhan gibun-eul neukkigo isseotda.
sentences.grammarNote:
**-아/어 서다** = standing and (sequential manner): **서서** 느끼고 있었다 (stood [there] and was feeling). **서다** (to stand) in the connective form **서서** describes the physical posture maintained throughout the main action: 줄을 서**서** 기다렸다 (stood in line and waited). It grounds the abstract feeling of peace in the concrete physical act of standing still at that spot.
**느끼고 있었다** progressive of perception: **느끼고 있었다** (was feeling). While Korean verbs of perception (느끼다, 보다, 듣다) can describe instantaneous experience with a simple past, **느끼고 있었다** emphasises the sustained, ongoing nature of the feeling — he was not merely experiencing a flash of peace but dwelling in it for an extended time, consistent with **오랫동안** (for a long time).

sentences.base_language: On the way down, his knees began to ache and he had to slow down on the steeper rocky sections.
sentences.target_language: 내려오는 길에 무릎이 아파 오기 시작해서, 더 가파른 바위 구간에서는 속도를 늦춰야 했다.
sentences.romanized: Naeryeoneun gil-e mureup-i apa ogi sijakhaeseo, deo gapareun bawi gugan-eseo-neun sokdo-reul neutchwoya haetda.
sentences.grammarNote:
**-아/어야 하다** obligation/necessity: 늦춰**야 했다** (had to slow down). **-아/어야 하다** expresses that an action is necessary or obligatory: 공부**해야 한다** (must study), 먹**어야 해요** (have to eat). Combined with the past tense **했다** it shows that the necessity was real and acted upon in a past situation — the aching knees made slowing down unavoidable.
**아파 오다** = to begin to ache, to come to hurt: 무릎이 **아파 오기 시작했다** (the knees began to ache). **아프다** (to be painful) + **오다** (to come): **-아/어 오다** here describes a sensation that starts at a distance and gradually moves in — approaching rather than sudden: 추위가 **밀려 온다** (the cold rolls in), 피로가 **몰려 온다** (fatigue comes flooding in). It gives the onset of pain an organic, creeping quality.

sentences.base_language: Back at the trailhead as the sun set, he sat down and pulled off his hiking boots with a long sigh of relief.
sentences.target_language: 해가 질 무렵 다시 등산로 입구로 돌아와서, 그는 자리에 앉아 긴 안도의 한숨을 내쉬면서 등산화를 벗어 버렸다.
sentences.romanized: Hae-ga jil mureop dasi deungsanno ipgu-ro dorawaseo, geu-neun jari-e anja gin ando-eui hanseum-eul naeswiimyeonseo deungsanhwa-reul beoseo beoryeotda.
literal: around the time the sun was setting
returned to the trailhead again-and so,
while letting out a long sigh of relief-while
pulled off his hiking boots (with finality).
sentences.grammarNote:
**-(으)ㄹ 무렵** approximate time 'around when': 해가 질 **무렵** (around the time the sun was setting). **무렵** is a temporal noun meaning 'around the time of' and is always preceded by a modifier: 도착할 **무렵** (around the time of arriving), 저녁 **무렵** (around evening time). It is slightly more literary than **때쯤** and suits the unhurried, descriptive pace of the scene at day's end.
**벗어 버리다** completion with relief: 등산화를 **벗어 버렸다** (pulled off the boots — and was finally done with them). **벗다** (to take off) + **-아/어 버리다** (completion with finality): **-아/어 버리다** here carries a tone of welcome relief and decisiveness — the boots came off completely and he was glad to be rid of them: 짐을 내려놓**아 버렸다** (set the burden down and was done with it).

sentences.base_language: Driving home with tired legs and a calm full heart, he thought he absolutely needed to do this far more often.
sentences.target_language: 지친 다리와 잔잔하고 충만한 마음을 안고 집으로 운전하면서, 이런 날을 훨씬 더 자주 만들어야겠다고 생각했다.
sentences.romanized: Jichin dari-wa janjanhago chungmanhan maeum-eul ango jib-euro unjeonhamyeonseo, ireon nal-eul hwolssin deo jaju mandeureoyagessda-go saenggakhaetda.
literal: 지친 다리와 잔잔하고 충만한 마음을 안고
holding tired legs and a calm full heart (embracing)
sentences.grammarNote:
**-아/어야겠다** firm personal resolution: **만들어야겠다** (must make / absolutely should make). **-아/어야 하다** (necessity) + **-겠-** (speaker's intention/realisation) → **-아/어야겠다**: a strong first-person resolution arising from the present experience: 열심히 해야**겠다** (I really must work hard), 일찍 자야**겠다** (I really should sleep early). It captures the feeling of renewed conviction that often comes at the end of a meaningful experience.
**마음을 안고** = carrying [a feeling] in one's heart, holding [an emotion]: **충만한 마음을 안고** 운전하면서 (while driving, carrying a full heart). **안다** (to hold/embrace) used figuratively: **꿈을 안고** 살다 (live holding a dream). It is a common Korean literary idiom for bearing an emotion as if physically carrying it — more embodied and warm than simply having a feeling. Combined with **-면서** it enriches the final image of Minho driving home suffused with contentment.
